reasoning: To resolve as YES, the Carnegie Global Protest Tracker must add a new entry for South Korea with a start date after the question's launch (roughly mid-January 2025) and a participant size of 10,000+. The main obstacle is how the tracker groups events: the massive, ongoing political unrest in South Korea was sparked by the December 2024 martial law declaration, meaning these protests will very likely have a late 2024 start date in the tracker. Similarly, the doctors' strike relates to an issue that began in early 2024. While new protests could theoretically emerge (e.g., around the May 2025 presidential election), Carnegie tends to group ongoing, related unrest into single narratives rather than logging multiple discrete entries. Furthermore, the tracker is typically updated only every few months, making it questionable whether a genuinely new 10,000+ protest from Spring 2025 would be cataloged and published by the July 1, 2025 resolution date. Thus, despite the high volume of protestors, the strict formatting constraints of the resolution source pull the probability down significantly.
batch_queries: Will the Global Protest Tracker report any protests in South Korea with 10,000+ participants, before July 1, 2025?
